title: ophrys subfusca subsp. aspea
intro: Ophrys subfusca subsp. aspea is a subspecies of the bee orchid, a type of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is found in the Iberian Peninsula and is known for its unique appearance and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Ophrys subfusca subsp. aspea is often used in infusions or decoctions, and can also be dried and used in teas or capsules.
